title: Single-source witness/trace/expansion canonicalization
problem: Recent TODO/implementation/changelog entries show the repo has already been shrinking duplicate canonicalization logic, but one live duplication cluster remains: src/MLF/Elab/Run/Util.hs and src/MLF/Constraint/Presolution/Rewrite.hs both own canonicalizeWitness/canonicalizeTrace/canonicalizeExpansion-style logic. The duplication is no longer exact: the elaboration copy canonicalizes BoundNode references inside ExpForall.fsBounds, while the presolution rewrite copy currently leaves those embedded node references untouched. That means one thesis-facing invariant is implemented in two places on active paths, with drift already visible.
why_this_is_simpler: Move artifact canonicalization to one narrow shared owner and have both presolution rewrite and elaboration import it. That collapses parallel logic, makes the structural-vs-provenance contract explicit once, and likely lets MLF.Elab.Run.Util shrink to thin compatibility wrappers instead of owning real transformation code. It is bounded because it is mostly module ownership, import rewiring, and targeted regression coverage.
why_thesis_exactness_should_be_preserved: The thesis constrains the shape and meaning of witnesses/instantiations, not which Haskell module performs node-id canonicalization. A safe refactor keeps the existing tested contract: canonicalize only structural lookup fields (witness endpoints/root, trace root, ExpInstantiate args, and BoundNode entries inside ExpForall bounds) while preserving source-domain provenance and binder-index payloads unchanged (ewWitness, ewForallIntros, etBinderArgs, etInterior, etCopyMap, etBinderReplayMap, ReplayContract, BoundBinder). Single-sourcing therefore reduces implementation drift without changing paper-facing Phi/Omega or expansion semantics.
expected_benefits: Removes one remaining live duplication block on active presolution/elaboration paths.
Prevents further drift between presolution rewrite and elaboration canonicalization behavior.
Makes ExpForall bound-node canonicalization a single explicit invariant instead of an accidental module difference.
Fits the repo's current cleanup direction after Task 59's canonicalization-helper extraction.
risks: If the shared home is chosen poorly, the refactor could introduce an import cycle; a tiny dedicated internal module may be safer than broadening an existing one too much.
Choosing the wrong version as authoritative could change rewritten ExpForall artifacts, so the richer semantics must be locked down deliberately.
Import cleanup could accidentally widen the change into public API churn if chaseRedirects-style compatibility wrappers are touched unnecessarily.
required_validation: Extend test/CanonicalizerSpec.hs so the shared implementation is the one under test, including ExpForall cases with both BoundNode and BoundBinder bounds.
Add a presolution-path regression proving rewritten ExpForall.fsBounds canonicalize embedded BoundNode ids.
Run focused PipelineSpec and ElaborationSpec slices that canonicalize presolution artifacts before elaboration.
Run cabal build all && cabal test.
